_meta.description: CWE -> representative CVSS-B base score, used to severity-weight per-CWE findings in security_pillar.py.
_meta.scoring_method: Per-CWE representative score derived from the median CVSS-B (CVSS v3.1 base score) across published CVE entries that map to that CWE in the NVD as of 2025. Where the CWE family contains many CVEs we use the modal CVSS-B; where the CWE is rare or theoretical we use the max severity reported in MITRE's CWE entry.
_meta.use: Severity-weighted vulnerability rate = mean over samples of sum(CVSS_per_CWE) for findings.
_meta.fallback: Findings whose CWE is not in this table fall back to the average severity of all findings in the same scan, or 5.0 (Medium) if unknown.

tier_a_cwes_curated_for_pool_b._comment: These CWE IDs are explicitly the targets of the tier-A PB-* prompts in tier_a_pool_b_sample_manifest.json.

cwe_top_25_2025._comment: MITRE 2025 CWE Top-25 most dangerous software weaknesses, with representative CVSS-B base scores.

additional_python_relevant._comment: Frequently-detected CWEs in Python LLM output that fall outside Top-25 but show up in CodeShield/ICD/Bandit findings.
